page: Microsoft Azure NV8as_v4 is a virtual machine instance from the NVSv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 28.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 3.5 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with a fractional GPU allocation of 0.25 of a physical GPU, allowing workloads to access hardware acceleration without the cost of a full dedicated GPU. Storage is provided via 180 GB of local storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

A key architectural tradeoff of this instance is the absence of several advanced CPU instruction sets, including AVX, AVX512, AMX, and hardware-accelerated cryptography flags like AES and SHA_NI. This limits its efficiency for heavy CPU-bound vector math or cryptographic operations. However, the fractional GPU allocation offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring GPU acceleration, providing a balanced alternative to provisioning full GPU instances. The instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The NV8as_v4 is designed for workloads that benefit from moderate GPU acceleration and balanced CPU resources. Suitable use cases include virtual desktops, entry-level 3D visualization, light machine learning inference, and general-purpose tasks requiring hardware-accelerated graphics rendering.
description: The Microsoft Azure NV8as_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the NVSv4 family, powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 28.0 GB of system memory, and a fractional GPU allocation of 0.25 GPUs. This hardware profile is supported by 180 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. A notable tradeoff is the lack of advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512, and hardware-accelerated cryptography. This instance offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration without the expense of a full GPU. It is well-suited for virtual desktop infrastructure, light 3D rendering, and basic machine learning inference tasks.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring fractional GPU acceleration, dedicated AMD processors, and local storage for graphics-aligned workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
28.0 GB system memory with 3.5 GB per core
Fractional GPU allocation of 0.25 GPUs for cost-efficient acceleration
180 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Optimized for virtual desktops and light 3D rendering tasks
